---
title: "Data-Mining-K-means"
format: html
---
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
BASE = r"C:\Users\piavi\OneDrive\Documents\Oracle Project\Data to use"

df = pd.read_csv(rf"{BASE}\Car_Crash_Fully_Preprocessed.csv")
```

```{python}
df.columns
```

set X and y
```{python}
y = df["Severity"]
X = df.drop(columns=["Severity"])
```

Because all 98 features are binary and sparse, apply PCA before clustering:
This makes clusters more meaningful and speeds up computation significantly.
```{python}
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
pca = PCA(n_components=10)  # retain ~80–90% variance
X_reduced = pca.fit_transform(X)
```


Finding Optimal K
```{python}
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

inertias = []
K_range = range(2, 11)

for k in K_range:
    km = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=42, n_init=10)
    km.fit(X_reduced)
    inertias.append(km.inertia_)

plt.plot(K_range, inertias, marker='o')
plt.xlabel('Number of clusters (K)')
plt.ylabel('Inertia')
plt.title('Elbow Method')
plt.show()
```
"Elbow is K = 3"

Fit the Model
```{python}
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

km = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=42, n_init=10)
df['Cluster'] = km.fit_predict(X_reduced)
```


Profiling 
```{python}
df.groupby('Cluster')[['Severity', 'Highway', 'ClearWeather', 'Daylight',
                         'CrashType_A', 'ViolCat_1']].mean()
```


Validate with Silhouette Score
```{python}
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score

score = silhouette_score(X_reduced, df['Cluster'], sample_size=10000)
print(f"Silhouette Score: {score:.3f}")
```
Silhouette Score: 2.04
A score closer to 1.0 is better; above 0.2 is generally acceptable for real-world data.
